title: The One with All the Jealousy

jokes.text: Does everybody hate these shoes? - Yeah. But don't worry. Nobody's gonna focus on that if you wear that towel-dress.
jokes.setup: Character asks if everyone hates their shoes, expecting reassurance
jokes.punchline: Instead of reassurance, friend escalates by insulting the dress too
jokes.explanation: Classic escalation structure works well - instead of helping, makes it worse. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: It's her first day at this new job. You're not supposed to start with her.
jokes.setup: Context suggests someone is being overly critical or harsh
jokes.punchline: The implication that they'll resume being mean after the first day
jokes.explanation: Reveals character dynamic - temporary mercy that implies future cruelty.

jokes.text: Botanists are such geeks. Is that a dinosaur tie?
jokes.setup: Character complains about botanists being geeky
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals their own geeky obsession with dinosaurs
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic self-awareness failure - character is exactly what they're mocking. Economical and well-structured.

jokes.text: It must take you forever to find your keys.
jokes.setup: Rachel has many purses
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's practical observation about the inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Standard observational humor, fits Phoebe's straightforward personality.

jokes.text: Please want the one with the turtles. - No, turtles scare me. I don't need that today.
jokes.setup: Phoebe wants Rachel to choose turtle purse
jokes.punchline: Rachel's specific fear of turtles and decision she doesn't need that stress
jokes.explanation: Absurd specific fear combined with practical life management. 'I don't need that today' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Is this a lunch box? - No, it's a purse. And there's a thermos in it.
jokes.setup: Someone questions what appears to be professional accessory
jokes.punchline: Rachel's defensive explanation that makes it sound more like a lunch box
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy about inappropriate work accessories. The thermos detail makes it worse.

jokes.text: A stripper at a bachelor party. That is so cliché. Why don't you guys get a magician?
jokes.setup: Character criticizes strippers as cliché entertainment
jokes.punchline: Suggests magician as alternative, setting up next joke
jokes.explanation: Sets up the following joke well but is fairly standard setup humor.

jokes.text: Well, if a magician can open my beer with his butt cheeks, then all right.
jokes.setup: Previous suggestion of magician instead of stripper
jokes.punchline: Chandler's crude but specific requirement for magical entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler-esque escalation with specific absurd imagery. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Don't cry out loud.
jokes.setup: Context of hiding feelings and keeping emotions inside
jokes.punchline: Reference to the song title as advice
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference delivered deadpan. Economical but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Guess who has an audition for a Broadway musical? - I wanna say you, but it seems like such an easy answer.
jokes.setup: Joey's obvious leading question about himself
jokes.punchline: Chandler acknowledging the obviousness of the setup
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about obvious setups, fits Chandler's awareness and Joey's self-centeredness.

jokes.text: So I think I'm gonna sing 'New York, New York' and 'I Left My Heart in San Francisco.'
jokes.setup: Joey preparing for A Tale of Two Cities audition
jokes.punchline: His literal interpretation of 'two cities' with famous city songs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - missing the point entirely but with confidence. Shows his lack of literary knowledge.

jokes.text: Joey, I don't think you get to pick the cities. - What? - Mr. Dickens gets to pick them.
jokes.setup: Joey's misunderstanding about choosing cities
jokes.punchline: Chandler explaining Dickens chose them, Joey's confusion
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Joey's literary ignorance and Chandler's attempts to help.

jokes.text: Who? - I'll get you the Cliff Notes. - The what? - The abridgment. - The what?
jokes.setup: Joey doesn't know who Dickens is
jokes.punchline: Escalating confusion as Chandler tries increasingly simple explanations
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating ignorance. Perfect character work - Joey gets dumber, Chandler gets more desperate. Well-structured.

jokes.text: Rachel Greene's line. How may I help you? - Hi, is Rachel there? - And who may I say is calling? - This is Ross. - Ross of...?
jokes.setup: Mark answers Rachel's phone professionally
jokes.punchline: Mark pretending not to know who Ross is despite being the boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Mark's passive-aggressive territory marking and Ross's growing paranoia.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! What did I just do? I think I just shipped 3000 bras to Personnel! - Honey, I gotta go! Mark, I need you!
jokes.setup: Rachel learning her new job
jokes.punchline: Major mistake followed by calling for Mark instead of continuing with Ross
jokes.explanation: Workplace comedy escalating Ross's fears. The specific nature of the mistake (bras to Personnel) adds to the humor.

jokes.text: All right, I just got changed in 30 seconds so you could be alone with him. You better go for it.
jokes.setup: Monica quickly changing clothes
jokes.punchline: Her aggressive wingwoman approach and time pressure
jokes.explanation: Monica's aggressive helper personality and the pressure she puts on others for romance.

jokes.text: You're a poet, and you don't know it. - Actually, I am a poet. - So you do know it.
jokes.setup: Monica uses the classic rhyming phrase
jokes.punchline: Julio reveals he actually is a poet, Monica's logical follow-up
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of a tired phrase with perfect logical follow-through. Economical and well-structured.

jokes.text: I could write an epic poem about this lip. - How would that go?
jokes.setup: Julio's over-the-top romantic poetry about Monica's lip
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical request to hear the actual poem
jokes.explanation: Monica's practical response to pretentious romance. Good contrast between characters.

jokes.text: Well, it didn't rhyme... but I liked it.
jokes.setup: Julio presumably recites his lip poem
jokes.punchline: Monica's polite but honest reaction to bad poetry
jokes.explanation: Monica trying to be supportive while acknowledging the poem's obvious flaws. Good character moment.

jokes.text: 'Three years of modern dance with Twyla Tharp'? 'Five years with the American Ballet Theatre'?
jokes.setup: Someone reading Joey's fake resume
jokes.punchline: Increasingly impressive and false dance credentials
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Joey's lies with specific, prestigious dance credentials that make his actual abilities funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, everybody lies on their résumé, okay? I wasn't one of the Zoom kids either.
jokes.setup: Joey's dance credentials are revealed as lies
jokes.punchline: His defense includes lying about being a Zoom kid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - defending lies with more lies. The Zoom kids reference is both specific and absurd.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, no, no. - What is that? - Sure, it looks stupid now. There's no music playing.
jokes.setup: Joey attempting to dance
jokes.punchline: His defensive explanation for why his dancing looks ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Joey's inability to accept his dance skills are terrible. Good physical comedy setup with character-driven denial.

jokes.text: Let me ask you this. What do you do for the extra hundred? - So would I have to provide the grapes?
jokes.setup: Chandler talking to a stripper service
jokes.punchline: His specific question about grape provision for unknown sexual service
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity about sexual services. The grape detail makes it both funny and innocent.

jokes.text: Get out! I couldn't stop if a meteor hit me.
jokes.setup: Monica reacting to Julio stopping mid-fooling-around to write poetry
jokes.punchline: Her hyperbolic comparison to meteor impact
jokes.explanation: Monica's sexual enthusiasm contrasted with Julio's artistic priorities. Meteor metaphor is absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: Sure, you name a kid that, what do you expect them to grow up to be?
jokes.setup: Stripper name 'Crystal Chandelier' mentioned
jokes.punchline: Chandler's observation about deterministic naming
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about nominative determinism. Fits Chandler's analytical personality.

jokes.text: My vessel so lovely, with nothing inside? Now that I've touched you, you seem emptier still? He thinks Monica's empty. She's the empty vase!
jokes.setup: Reading Julio's poem about an empty vase
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's realization that Monica is being insulted
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Monica's confrontation. Phoebe's literal interpretation of metaphorical poetry drives plot forward.

jokes.text: Monica's the vase, right? - You are such a mystery to me.
jokes.setup: Joey weighing in on the poetry interpretation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's exasperation with Joey's unpredictable intelligence
jokes.explanation: Joey surprisingly gets something right after his earlier literary ignorance. Chandler's reaction is perfect.

jokes.text: It's a Love Bug. - Somebody wants people to know you have a boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Ross sends Rachel flowers at work
jokes.punchline: Mark's analysis of Ross's territorial behavior
jokes.explanation: Mark correctly identifying Ross's jealousy tactics. Sets up bigger gesture to come.

jokes.text: Congratulations on your first week At your brand-new job It won't be long Before you're the boss And you know who will be there To support you Your one and only boyfriend It's nice to have a boyfriend! Your loyal, loving boyfriend, Ross!
jokes.setup: Ross sends flowers, now escalates further
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top barbershop quartet emphasizing 'boyfriend' repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Ross's jealousy into public embarrassment. Repetition of 'boyfriend' makes territorial marking obvious.

jokes.text: You might as well have just come in and peed all around my desk!
jokes.setup: Rachel angry about Ross's territorial behavior
jokes.punchline: Her crude but accurate animal behavior comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect metaphor for territorial behavior. Crude but accurate, and very quotable.

jokes.text: I would never do that.
jokes.setup: Rachel's accusation about peeing around her desk
jokes.punchline: Ross's literal, defensive response missing the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Ross's literal-mindedness creates humor. Perfect timing and economy.

jokes.text: How'd it go? - Knocked over a girl.
jokes.setup: Asking about Joey's dance class
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact report of disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and Joey's matter-of-fact delivery of embarrassing failure.

jokes.text: Why don't you send her a musical bug? - No, you already did that.
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting what Ross should do about Mark
jokes.punchline: Chandler pointing out Ross already escalated past that
jokes.explanation: Simple callback showing escalation pattern. Functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: And pretty soon you'll be like: 'Hi.' And, 'Well, I can't go. Rachel and Mark might be there.' And we'll be like, 'Man, get over it! It's been four years!'
jokes.setup: Chandler painting picture of Ross losing Rachel to Mark
jokes.punchline: Specific future scenario with awkward small talk and friend frustration
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with specific dialogue predictions. Chandler's paranoid scenario feels realistic and funny.

jokes.text: Wait, I've got something for you. - Mark. - It's okay. Rachel knows. - Yeah, but even so.
jokes.setup: Workplace interaction that seems professional
jokes.punchline: Dialogue suggesting inappropriate romantic relationship
jokes.explanation: Misdirection that makes audience think Mark is being inappropriate, setting up Ross's misunderstanding.

jokes.text: I can't help it. I'm just crazy about you. Come on, no one's around. Just kiss me.
jokes.setup: Previous ambiguous workplace interaction
jokes.punchline: Mark seemingly making inappropriate advances on Rachel
jokes.explanation: Continues misdirection before reveal. Audience shares Ross's interpretation momentarily.

jokes.text: All right, that's it! Get off of her! - What is going on? - What's going on? I'll tell you what's going on!
jokes.setup: Ross bursting in on what he thinks is inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: His dramatic intervention in innocent situation
jokes.explanation: Ross's paranoia finally exploding into action. Physical comedy of misunderstanding.

jokes.text: What do I have to do to get some service?
jokes.setup: Ross's dramatic entrance and confrontation
jokes.punchline: Reveal that he's actually just a frustrated customer needing a tie
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection reveal. Retroactively makes his 'intervention' about customer service, not romance.

jokes.text: It's hand, hand... head, head... up, pas de bourrée, pas de bourrée, big turn here, rond de jambe. Slide, step, step... and jazz hands!
jokes.setup: Professional dance instruction with technical terms
jokes.punchline: Ending with simple 'jazz hands' after complex ballet terminology
jokes.explanation: Contrast between sophisticated ballet and simple jazz hands. Sets up Joey's upcoming disaster.

jokes.text: It's steppity-step and jazz hands.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to simplify the complex dance instruction
jokes.punchline: His childish reduction of professional choreography
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey simplification. 'Steppity-step' is childish and memorable.

jokes.text: So I'm just an empty vase? - What? - So, I don't read as many important books as you do. And I don't write trick poems that aren't actually what they seem.
jokes.setup: Monica confronting Julio about the poem
jokes.punchline: Her honest self-assessment and criticism of pretentious poetry
jokes.explanation: Monica defending herself against intellectual snobbery. 'Trick poems' is a good phrase.

jokes.text: You have no right to make that kind of judgment! You don't even know me!
jokes.setup: Monica's building anger about being judged
jokes.punchline: Her passionate defense of her worth and interests
jokes.explanation: Monica's righteous anger peaks. More dramatic than funny but necessary character moment.

jokes.text: The poem is not about you. - What? - 'The Empty Vase' is not about you. It's about all women. Well, all American women.
jokes.setup: Julio clarifying the poem isn't about Monica specifically
jokes.punchline: His expansion to insulting all American women instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - instead of making it better, Julio makes it infinitely worse by generalizing the insult.

jokes.text: What was that? - I know. It's the best I could get out of them.
jokes.setup: Someone reacting to terrible group dance performance
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact acceptance of the disaster he caused
jokes.explanation: Joey's inability to recognize his own incompetence. Deadpan delivery of failure.

jokes.text: Yeah, look what I got! See, she's fully dressed, right? And then you click it, and she's naked! She's a business woman, walking down the street. She's window shopping, and she's naked!
jokes.setup: Ross showing off bachelor party novelty pen
jokes.punchline: His overly detailed and enthusiastic explanation of the clicking pen
jokes.explanation: Ross's unexpected enthusiasm for juvenile humor. The detailed narrative makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You know what? I'm gonna spend some alone time with the pen.
jokes.setup: Ross's enthusiastic demonstration of the naked pen
jokes.punchline: Chandler's euphemistic request for the pen
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler euphemism delivered deadpan. Economy and character voice are excellent.

jokes.text: Okay, Ross, this pen's getting kind of boring. Can you pick me up some porn?
jokes.setup: Chandler's earlier request for 'alone time' with the pen
jokes.punchline: His casual, matter-of-fact request for pornography escalation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and escalation. Chandler's casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You have a play-date with a stripper? - Man, I gotta get a kid!
jokes.setup: Ross having innocent play-date with stripper and her kid
jokes.punchline: Chandler's completely wrong reason for wanting children
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and Chandler logic. Economy is excellent, completely wrong motivation for parenthood.

jokes.text: I mean, doesn't she, you know, have any other stripper-mom friends of her own?
jokes.setup: Rachel's jealousy about Ross's play-date
jokes.punchline: Her assumption that strippers have their own social network of stripper-moms
jokes.explanation: Great absurd specificity. 'Stripper-mom friends' is a funny compound concept.

jokes.text: Either that or you just turned him on and sent him to a stripper.
jokes.setup: Rachel giving Ross passionate kiss before his play-date
jokes.punchline: Chandler's observation about the poor timing
jokes.explanation: Chandler's dark humor about the situation. Good observation about unintended consequences.

jokes.text: Oh, no. Chandler, please don't. No, I beg of you to not...
jokes.setup: Chandler about to reveal his jealousy
jokes.punchline: Rachel's increasingly desperate attempt to stop him from admitting it
jokes.explanation: Rachel's desperation builds comedy through escalating pleading. Sets up Chandler's admission.

jokes.text: Mr. Pretentious, You think there's no one finer, Your poems are unpublished, And you work in a diner, You're no God's gift to women, That's all in your head, You are just a butt-munch, No one likes a butt-munch! And you're also bad in bed
jokes.setup: Monica getting revenge on Julio with barbershop quartet
jokes.punchline: Increasingly harsh personal attacks delivered in cheerful barbershop style
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback escalation. Contrast between cheerful delivery and harsh content is brilliant. 'Butt-munch' repeated is memorable.

tmdb_overview: Ross becomes increasingly jealous of Rachel's new colleague, Mark, and spies on her. Monica hooks up with a co-worker, who writes her a poem with a hidden meaning.
